entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A major cereal crop and dietary staple, scientifically known as Sorghum bicolor L. Moench, exhibiting great phenotypic variability.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A region where sorghum is a dietary staple for millions of people.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A region where sorghum is a dietary staple for millions of people.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A yield character of sorghum used for genetic analysis.

entities.name: QUALITY TRAITS
entities.description: Quality characters of sorghum used for genetic analysis.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The variation in genetic makeup among individuals of a species, assessed for yield and quality characters.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The observable physical differences among cultivated sorghum varieties.

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at broadening the gene pool, characterizing germplasm, and selecting parental genotypes.

entities.name: GENE POOL
entities.description: The total genetic diversity available within a crop species.

entities.name: GERMPLASM COLLECTIONS
entities.description: Collections of genetic resources used for characterization in breeding programs.

entities.name: PARENTAL GENOTYPES
entities.description: Specific genotypes chosen as parents in breeding programs.

entities.name: BROAD-SENSE HERITABILITY
entities.description: A measure of the proportion of phenotypic variation that is due to genetic factors.
